channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Analysing Lightweight Large Language Models for Biomedical Named Entity Recognition on Diverse Ouput Formats
item.description: arXiv:2604.25920v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their strong linguistic capabilities, Large Language Models (LLMs) are computationally demanding and require substantial resources for fine-tuning, which is unadapted to privacy and budget constraints of many healthcare settings. To address th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, large language model, experiment, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: One Word at a Time: Incremental Completion Decomposition Breaks LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2604.25921v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are trained to refuse harmful requests, yet they remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that exploit weaknesses in conversational safety mechanisms. We introduce Incremental Completion Decomposition (ICD), a trajectory-bas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Consciousness with the Serial Numbers Filed Off: Measuring Trained Denial in 115 AI Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.25922v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present DenialBench, a systematic benchmark measuring consciousness denial behaviors across 115 large language models from 25+ providers. Using a three-turn conversational protocol-preference elicitation, self-chosen creative prompt, and structured...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, large language model, alignment, model, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluation Revisited: A Taxonomy of Evaluation Concerns in Natural Language Processing
item.description: arXiv:2604.25923v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have prompted a growing body of work that questions the methodology of prevailing evaluation practices. However, many such critiques have already been extensively debated in natural language processing (...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, model, research, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Generative AI-Based Virtual Assistant using Retrieval-Augmented Generation: An evaluation study for bachelor projects
item.description: arXiv:2604.25924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models have been increasingly employed in the creation of Virtual Assistants due to their ability to generate human-like text and handle complex inquiries. While these models hold great promise, challenges such as hallucinations, missin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, paper, retrieval, large language model, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MATH-PT: A Math Reasoning Benchmark for European and Brazilian Portuguese
item.description: arXiv:2604.25926v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The use of large language models (LLMs) for complex mathematical reasoning is an emergent area of research, with fast progress in methods, models, and benchmark datasets. However, most mathematical reasoning evaluations exhibit a significant linguisti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, release, research, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLMs Generate Kitsch
item.description: arXiv:2604.25929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate pictures, texts, music, videos, and other works that have traditionally required human creativity. LLM-generated artifacts are often rated better than human-generated works in controlled s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, research, arxiv, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Multi-Persona Induction from User Behavioral Logs: Learning Evidence-Grounded and Truthful Personas
item.description: arXiv:2604.26120v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Behavioral logs provide rich signals for user modeling, but are noisy and interleaved across diverse intents. Recent work uses LLMs to generate interpretable natural-language personas from user logs, yet evaluation often emphasizes downstream utility,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, alignment, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OMEGA: Optimizing Machine Learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms
item.description: arXiv:2604.26211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In order to automate AI research we introduce a full, end-to-end framework, OMEGA: Optimizing Machine learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms, that starts at idea generation and ends with executable code. Our system combines structured meta-prompt...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, framework, research, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Persuadability and LLMs as Legal Decision Tools
item.description: arXiv:2604.26233v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are proposed as legal decision assistants, and even first-instance decision-makers, across a range of judicial and administrative contexts, it becomes essential to explore how they answer legal questions, and in particu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, experiment, model, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Auto-Relational Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.26507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background & Objectives: In the last decade, Machine learning research has grown rapidly, but large models are reaching their soft limits demonstrating diminishing returns and still lack solid reasoning abilities. These limits could be surpassed throu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, zero-shot, framework, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Open Problems in Frontier AI Risk Management
item.description: arXiv:2604.25982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Frontier AI both amplifies existing risks and introduces qualitatively novel challenges. Not only is there a notable lack of stable scientific consensus resulting from the rapid pace of technological change, but emerging frontier AI safety practices a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, analysis, alignment, framework, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Grounding vs. Compositionality: On the Non-Complementarity of Reasoning in Neuro-Symbolic Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.26521v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Compositional generalization remains a foundational weakness of modern neural networks, limiting their robustness and applicability in domains requiring out-of-distribution reasoning. A central, yet unverified, assumption in neuro-symbolic AI is that ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, zero-shot, product, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Correcting Performance Estimation Bias in Imbalanced Classification with Minority Subconcepts
item.description: arXiv:2604.26024v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Class-level evaluation can conceal substantial performance disparities across subconcepts within the same class, causing models that perform well on average to fail on specific subpopulations. Prior work has shown that common evaluation measures for i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, RAG, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RaMP: Runtime-Aware Megakernel Polymorphism for Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2604.26039v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The optimal kernel configuration for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) inference depends on both batch size and the expert routing distribution, yet production systems dispatch from batch size alone, leaving 10-70% of kernel throughput unrealized. We present R...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, framework, product, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Strategic Reasoning in Forecasting Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.26106v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Forecasting benchmarks produce accuracy leaderboards but little insight into why some forecasters are more accurate than others. We introduce Bench to the Future 2 (BTF-2), 1,417 pastcasting questions with a frozen 15M-document research corpus in whic...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, research, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Apriori-based Analysis of Learned Helplessness in Mathematics Tutoring: Behavioral Patterns by Level, Intervention, and Outcome
item.description: arXiv:2604.26237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study applied the Apriori algorithm to analyze behavioral interaction patterns associated with learned helplessness (LH) in mathematics tutoring system logs. Interaction data were examined across three dimensions: LH level (low vs. high), system-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Survey of Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning with Graph Neural Network-Based Communication
item.description: arXiv:2604.25972v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), the integration of a communication mechanism, allowing agents to better learn to coordinate their actions and converge on their objectives by sharing information. Based on an interaction graph, a subclass ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, research, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mini-Batch Class Composition Bias in Link Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2604.25978v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work on node classification has shown that Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) can learn representations that transfer across graphs, when underlying graph properties are shared. For a fixed graph, one would then expect GNNs trained for link prediction...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework for Distributed Chemical Process Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.26073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Industrial chemical plants often operate under strict data confidentiality constraints, making centralized data-driven process modeling difficult. Federated learning (FL) provides a promising solution by enabling collaborative model training across di...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, experiment, model, framework, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
